policy_name: Third-Party Security Policy - 7.3 Managing Changes to Third-party Services
evidence_text:
7.3 Managing Changes to Third-party Services

<<CLIENT>> shall consider the following aspects for information security and data protection review when managing changes to third-party services, at a minimum:

1. Changes to third-party agreements and/ or sub-contracting to another third-party.
2. Enhancements to the current services offered.
3. Development and/ use of new applications, systems, and technologies.
4. Modifications or updates of the organization’s policies and procedures.

policy_name: Information Security Incident Management Policy - 5.8 Learning from Information Security Incidents
evidence_text:
5.8 Learning from Information Security Incidents

The CSIRT shall:

1. Implement relevant mechanisms to enable the types, volumes, costs (liaising with Finance Team), and impacts of information security incidents to be quantified and monitored.
2. Utilize the information gained from evaluating information security incidents to identify recurring or high impact incidents and inform risk assessment and risk treatment activities.

5.8 Learning from Information Security Incidents

The CSIRT shall:

1. Implement relevant mechanisms to enable the types, volumes, costs (liaising with Finance Team), and impacts of information security incidents to be quantified and monitored.
2. Utilize the information gained from evaluating information security incidents to identify recurring or high impact incidents and inform risk assessment and risk treatment activities.
